Test factor associations with sample groups

Source: R/testFactorAssociations.R
testFactorAssociations.Rd

Tests whether MOFA factor scores are significantly associated with different sample groups using the Kruskal-Wallis test. Grouping variables can be specified explicitly or selected automatically from the sample metadata.

Usage

testFactorAssociations(object, factor = NULL, groups = NULL)

Arguments

object

A trained MOFA2 object containing factor scores and sample metadata.

factor

Character or integer(1). The MOFA factor to test (required).

groups

Character vector. Names of metadata columns to use as grouping variables. If NULL (default), the function automatically selects columns with 2–10 unique non-NA values.

Value

A data.frame with one row per tested grouping variable and columns:

group

Name of the grouping variable.

statistic

Kruskal-Wallis H statistic.

p_value

Raw p-value.

df

Degrees of freedom.

p_adjusted

Benjamini-Hochberg adjusted p-value.

Details

The function:

  1. Extracts factor scores for the specified factor from the MOFA object.

  2. Merges factor scores with sample metadata.

  3. Selects grouping variables (auto or user-specified).

  4. Runs Kruskal-Wallis tests and applies Benjamini-Hochberg correction.

When groups = NULL, columns with 2–10 unique non-NA values are selected (these typically represent categorical clinical variables such as Gleason grade, tumour stage, or treatment arm).

See also

kruskal.test, p.adjust

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Auto-select grouping variables
results <- testFactorAssociations(mofa_object, factor = 1)

# Test specific grouping variables
results <- testFactorAssociations(mofa_object,
                                  factor = "Factor1",
                                  groups = c("gleason_grade", "pathologic_stage"))

significant <- results[results$p_adjusted < 0.05, ]
} # }
